findings.description: Complete JWT/OIDC implementation with 6-layer defense-in-depth: JWT validation (ConfigureJwtBearerOptions 238 lines), claims-based tenant validation, RBAC with wildcard support, actor-based authorization, MediatR pipeline behaviors, DAPR ACL. Token validation enforces signature, expiry, issuer, audience with 1-min clock skew.

TLS via DAPR mTLS (SPIFFE certificates), HTTPS enforced in Admin.UI (HSTS + redirect), JWT RequireHttpsMetadata=true in production. Log sanitization prevents token/payload leakage. Terminology sanitization removes internal details from client responses.
recommendations: MINOR: Add HTTPS redirect middleware to EventStore Program.cs for defense-in-depth (currently relies on DAPR mTLS)

findings.description: Comprehensive input sanitization: ExtensionMetadataSanitizer detects XSS, SQLi, LDAP injection, path traversal, control characters. FluentValidation SubmitCommandRequestValidator (110 lines) enforces field-level constraints with regex patterns. MediatR-level SubmitCommandValidator provides second validation layer.

Dual-layer rate limiting: per-tenant (1000/min, sliding window) and per-consumer (100/sec, fixed window). 429 responses with Retry-After header and ProblemDetails. Health endpoints exempt. Per-tenant overrides via DAPR config store. Dynamic configuration sync.

User secrets for local dev (hexalith-eventstore), empty production defaults (overridden by env vars), development-only signing key clearly labeled 'DevOnly', minimum 32-char key validation, Keycloak OIDC for production (no symmetric keys needed).
Deny-by-default access control on all 3 sidecars. Per-app-id caller restrictions. Trust domain enforcement via SPIFFE certificates. Sample domain service has zero state store access (D4 principle).

priority_actions: MINOR: Add explicit HTTPS redirect middleware to EventStore service for defense-in-depth
OPTIONAL: Add security scanning (OWASP ZAP or Snyk) to CI pipeline
OPTIONAL: Implement secret rotation policies documentation
summary: Security posture is LOW risk. 6-layer defense-in-depth fully implemented: JWT auth, claims-based RBAC, multi-tenant isolation, dual-layer rate limiting, comprehensive input sanitization, DAPR ACL. No critical vulnerabilities found. Minor improvement: explicit HTTPS in EventStore (currently DAPR mTLS).
